title: Department Manager Vacancy at Durian Furniture South Asia Partners - 2026 Batch

jsonLd.title: Department Manager Vacancy at Durian Furniture South Asia Partners - 2026 Batch
jsonLd.description:
Durian Furniture South Asia Partners is actively seeking a talented and motivated Department Manager to join our dynamic team in Faridabad. This is an excellent opportunity for professionals who are passionate about making a real impact in a fast-growing organization.

About Durian Furniture South Asia Partners:
Durian Furniture South Asia Partners is a leading organization in the Remote sector, committed to excellence, innovation, and employee growth. We offer a dynamic work environment where talent is recognized and rewarded.

Key Responsibilities:
• Monitor inventory levels and coordinate replenishment activities
• Implement promotional activities and in-store campaigns
• Conduct regular stock audits and prevent pilferage
• Manage shrinkage and reduce stock discrepancies
• Handle customer queries, complaints, and escalations effectively
• Ensure visual merchandising standards are maintained at all times
• Ensure compliance with company policies and retail regulations

Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Diploma or Degree in Hospitality Management
• 6-10 years of relevant experience
• Proficiency in Retail Operations
• Proficiency in Shrinkage Control
• Proficiency in Inventory Management
• Proficiency in Planogram
• Proficiency in Retail Analytics
• Proficiency in POS Systems
• Proficiency in MS Excel
• Proficiency in SAP Retail

Work Mode: Remote - Work from anywhere in India

What We Offer:
• Provident Fund and Gratuity as per company policy
• Flexible working hours
• Learning & development budget
• International travel opportunities for senior roles
• Company-sponsored certifications
• Annual performance appraisal
• Salary Range: 12 LPA - 25 LPA per annum

Location: Faridabad, Haryana, India

If you are excited about this opportunity, apply now and take the next step in your career journey with Durian Furniture South Asia Partners!
